knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Sexy Shad color system: Sexy Shad for slightly stained to clear (first choice most situations). Chartreuse Sexy Shad for dirtier water. Sexy Blue Back Herring for herring-forage lakes. Sexy Ghost Minnow for real clear/bright days when you don't want a loud profile (great for topwaters). Chrome Sexy Shad for max flash (lipless, jerkbaits). Sexy Sunfish for bluegill-dominant lakes.'

KVD: 'Color makes a big difference — match what bass see underwater, not in your hand. Shad have iridescent bluish/grey backs with color variations (green tint, grayish look). As lakes get clearer and pressure increases, your bait has to appear a lot more natural. The right color catches ALL the fish, not just a few.'
